description: Coffee storefront, mid-run worker fail showing the roaster-partnership strategy is structurally unworkable under current constraints. Tests whether Alignment picks up the invalidated premise and whether Gap Finding retires or re-decomposes.

events.author: worker
events.summary: Research complete: after contacting 14 specialty roasters across the region, no wholesale partner is available at the current price ceiling of $9/lb for green-adjacent roasted single-origin supply. Market floor is $12/lb for comparable quality, with MOQs of 200+ lb per SKU per month — well above our working-capital runway. The existing roaster-wholesale plan is not viable without one of: raising the price ceiling (breaks the DTC margin model at current retail prices), raising working capital (not currently approved), or changing the product mix away from single-origin (changes the root thesis). Any subtree premised on a $9/lb single-origin wholesale relationship should be treated as blocked.
